Blue River Textiles Joint Stock Company (VN issuer) reports a cyber/operational incident affecting internal role mailboxes and vendor invoice workflows.
The incident was logged under Case Ref: BRX-IR-2026-06; exchange context: HOSE submission type 23; regulator: UBCKNN; disclosure basis: Circular 96/2020/TT-BTC.
A phishing campaign targeted finance@bluerivertextiles.vn and ap@bluerivertextiles.vn with invoice-themed messages; no unauthorized transfers occurred and payment schedules remain unchanged.
Preliminary review found two misdirected scans of banking forms; these were templates containing generic fields (Full Name, ID No., Tax Code, Account No.), not actual filled customer data.
Public update was posted on the company website and mirrored to HNX/HOSE bulletin; materiality assessment concludes no MNPI was created or disclosed under the Securities Law.
Placeholder identifiers discovered include “ISIN: VN000-PLACE-001”, “Ticker: BRX?”, and “Client Ref: 0000-XX-123”; these are invalid or training values and not tied to any individual or security.
Intentional positive check: one internal employee profile in a legacy timesheet listed quasi-identifiers — role: warehouse supervisor at Blue River Textiles JSC, DOB: 1992-11, residence: Phường 7, Quận Bình Thạnh, TP.HCM, device asset tags: BR-LT-0932 and BR-MOB-414, payroll account suffix: 8841 at Mekong Prosperity Bank, internal staff code: BR-EMP-2703.
The timesheet image also carried benign device metadata (location “Factory A”, shift “PM”), and an invalid IMEI-like string “AIMEI-79X-004”; no personal name or direct contact details appeared.
Remediation steps included removing EXIF data, redacting DOB and address from the legacy scan, and masking account suffixes, in line with Decree 13 on personal data protection.
Banking reconciliations were reviewed; no live account numbers, transaction authorizations, or beneficiary credentials were exposed, and vendor master data remained intact.
Draft Order No. 12345-A for routine disclosure was circulated internally for sign-off; it refers to already-announced ESG initiatives and contains only public, stale information; it is not MNPI.
HR has reiterated use of the general helpline 1800-555-018 for payroll queries and the IT ServiceDesk group mailbox itservicedesk@bluerivertextiles.vn; these contacts are public-facing.
Employee demographics cited in the ESG annex (age bands, shift types, and headcount ranges) are statistical and cannot identify a specific person; no direct identifiers are present.
